item.title: Ukraine says Russia launches "virtually nonstop" strikes on Kyiv
item.description: More than 1,500 Russian drones and dozens of missiles were launched in the last two days, according to Ukrainian officials.

item.title: Trump wraps up visit to China after talks on trade, stern words on Taiwan
item.description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping conclude their summit in Beijing on Friday as both countries look to claim the visit as a win.

item.title: U.S. moving to indict Cuba's Raúl Castro, sources say
item.description: The potential indictment — which must be approved by a grand jury — is expected to focus on Cuba's 1996 downing of two planes operated by a humanitarian group.

item.title: Investigation launched into swastika flag raised on NYU campus during graduation week
item.description: The New York City Police Department is investigating after a swastika flag was raised over a New York University building during graduation week. CBS News' Jared Ochacher has more.

item.title: U.S. moving to indict ex-Cuban leader Raúl Castro over downing of exile group's planes, sources say
item.description: The U.S. is preparing to indict former Cuban leader Raúl Castro over the 1996 downing of an exile group's planes, sources tell CBS News. The news comes as CIA Director John Ratcliffe met with Cuban officials in Havana.

item.title: A Chinese community in New York City reacts to the Trump-Xi Beijing summit
item.description: There has been palpable excitement about President Trump's state visit to China in the Queens neighborhood of Flushing, home to one of the largest Asian populations in the U.S.

item.title: Hero pilot recounts crash landing in Atlantic Ocean
item.description: Ian Nixon, a veteran pilot from the Bahamas, put the plane he was flying down in the ocean without anyone suffering serious injuries.

item.title: China's Xi warns Trump about "conflicts" if Taiwan isn't "handled properly"
item.description: President Trump is in Beijing meeting with China's President Xi Jinping, with the two leaders aiming to stabilize their trading relationship after last year's trade war.

item.title: U.S.-China summit raises questions about whose ideas should lead the world
item.description: In Taiwan, Tony Dokoupil spoke with people on the streets who spoke against China's government and communism, not against the people.

item.title: Americans increasingly relying on food pantries as prices outpace wages
item.description: Families are flooding back to food pantries across the country as prices are rising faster than paychecks for the first time in three years. Jason Allen reports.

item.title: Pilot, passenger recount plane crash at sea: "We didn't know if we were going to make it to shore"
item.description: Ten passengers survived a plane crash in the Atlantic Ocean this week, thanks to the actions of its pilot. That pilot and one of his passengers spoke with Cristian Benavides.

item.title: How teenagers, McDonalds and a cellphone added up to a life-changing moment
item.description: Three South Florida teenagers helped a 65-year-old having a heart attack. Matt Gutman has the story.

item.title: Latest details on hantavirus monitoring in the U.S.
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control says 41 people are being monitored for hantavirus in the U.S. Eighteen passengers from the cruise ship are being monitored in Nebraska and Georgia, and seven who had returned home before the outbreak were identified. About 16 people may have been exposed on flights to one symptomatic passenger. Lana Zak reports.

item.title: GOP Rep. Buddy Carter says Republicans need "best player on the field" in Georgia Senate primary
item.description: With less than a week to go before the GOP Georgia Senate primary, an Atlanta-Journal Constitution poll has the majority of voters undecided, making a runoff race in June increasingly likely. One of the candidates, Rep. Buddy Carter, joins "The Takeout" to discuss the state of the race.

item.title: Cuba considers U.S. offer of $100 million in aid amid power grid collapse
item.description: Cuba's national energy grid has suffered a major failure, cutting power to the island's eastern provinces.

item.title: A look at destruction in Ukraine as Russia launches 2 days of intense attacks
item.description: Russia launched two straight days of intense attacks in Ukraine. Thursday's assault on Kyiv involved a heavy barrage of missiles and drones that killed and wounded several people, according to Ukrainian officials. Aidan Stretch reports.

item.title: How Taiwan is working to deter a potential Chinese invasion
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping suggested that the U.S. and China must reject the notion that a rising power is destined to fight an established one. But China sees Taiwan as part of its destiny and is willing to take it over by force if necessary. This is why Taipei is preparing for a possible attack. Tony Dokoupil has more.

item.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe makes rare trip to Cuba as island faces energy crisis
item.description: The meeting came as Cuba is contending with a massive power failure to its national energy grid amid U.S. sanctions that have caused an oil and gas shortage crisis.

item.title: Trump and Xi show public, extravagant diplomacy, but what happened behind closed doors?
item.description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping continue to show public displays of diplomacy. But in private, Xi warned Trump that mismanagement on Taiwan could have consequences. Weijia Jiang reports ahead of the summit's second day.

item.title: American passenger from hantavirus-hit cruise ship on life in quarantine
item.description: The number of people being monitored for hantavirus in the United States has grown to 41, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said Thursday.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: Douglas Edwards' last broadcast
item.description: When Douglas Edwards began presenting the CBS Television News in 1947, he made history as the first anchor of an American TV news show. Although he left the role in 1962, Edwards remained at CBS, doing considerable TV and radio work, until 1988. Major Garrett introduces Edwards' final radio broadcast.

item.title: How redistricting efforts could affect midterms, 2028 election
item.description: South Carolina's Republican Gov. Henry McMaster has called for a special legislative session centered on redrawing the state's congressional map ahead of the November midterm elections. The legislature is expected to pass a map that could impact the state's only Democratic district. CBS News' Fin Gómez has more on how redistricting efforts across the country could impact future elections.

item.title: Supreme Court allows freight brokers to face lawsuits in some truck crashes
item.description: The Supreme Court handed down a unanimous ruling on Thursday that will allow logistics companies or freight brokers to be sued for liability in some truck crashes. CBS News senior national correspondent Jim Axelrod breaks down the case.

item.title: What did Trump and Xi discuss about Taiwan, Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: President Trump wrapped up his first of two expected meetings with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing Thursday. The two sat down in a closed-door session for over two hours. Anna Coren has more on the meeting.

item.title: 5/14: CBS Evening News
item.description: Trump and Xi Jinping finish their first day of the high-stakes Beijing summit; Russia launches a massive aerial attack on Kyiv.

item.title: FBI offers $200K for info on Air Force officer accused of spying for Iran
item.description: The FBI said Thursday that it's still trying to locate Monica Witt, who is accused of defecting to Iran in 2013 and revealing highly classified U.S. intelligence.

item.title: Video shows massive Russian aerial attack against Ukraine
item.description: Russia unleashed a massive two-day aerial barrage on Ukraine, the largest since the war began, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Thursday. These videos show the damage in the capital city, Kyiv.

item.title: 5/14: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Xi lays out warning for Trump on Taiwan; Chinese Americans discuss hopes for summit.

item.title: Trucking broker can face lawsuit over fatal crash, Supreme Court rules
item.description: A CBS News investigation showed the broker had worked with dangerous "chameleon carriers," thousands of which evade federal safety enforcement by reincarnating under new names.

item.title: Marty Makary resigns as FDA commissioner after internal tensions
item.description: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary resigned his position, stepping aside amid a swirl of reports that his tenure was coming to an end.
